audit_items.proof_trace: Both overloads lack internal null guards, but proof holds via caller guarantee: the sole call site (service_pool.h:404) passes `name.c_str()` where `name` is `const std::string&`, and `std::string::c_str()` always returns non-null. The `(const char*)` overload has zero callers in the codebase. An attacker supplying an empty service name via RPC causes `murmurhash("", 0)` — the while loop is skipped (data==end) and switch(0) hits default:break, no pointer dereference occurs.
